title: Blaberopus lucidus
taxonomy: Blaberopus lucidus is classified as a species of stick insect within the family Heteropterygidae, which is a family of stick insects commonly found in Southeast Asia. This classification places Blaberopus lucidus within the order Mantodea, which comprises all stick insects and mantises. The order Mantodea is further divided into several suborders, with Blaberopus lucidus belonging to the suborder Eumantodea. This suborder is characterized by the presence of long, slender antennae and the ability to jump or fly. The classification of Blaberopus lucidus reflects its unique adaptations and characteristics as a species of stick insect.
